div.head: The Minister in Salvador (Schuyler) to the Secretary of State

opener.dateline:
San Salvador, December 16, 1921—1 p.m.
[Received December 17—10:40 a.m.]

div.p:
68. I saw the President this morning and left with him a memorandum embodying your December 14, 5 p.m.Not printed; see telegram no. 43, Dec. 14, to the Chargé in Nicaragua, p. 161. He was greatly
pleased and will publish it locally to strengthen position he has taken, stating that under no circumstances would he interfere by force in Guatemala or to impose a federation.

He states that policy of non-interference is approved by all elements of Salvadorean people except so-called Unionists in Santa Ana and he hopes your declaration [will] quiet latter for the time at least. Government has situation well in hand.
